newsSinceJun6: No new material fundamentals since June 6. The major catalysts all pre-date the window: FQ4 FY26 blowout on June 1 (revenue $437M vs guide, EPS $1.16 vs $1.02 est, FY26 revenue $1.34B up ~206% y/y), FQ1 FY27 guide $465-475M with management forecasting >80% y/y FY27 growth, a wave of analyst PT raises June 2 (Mizuho $290, Roth $300, JPMorgan $250, TD Cowen $260), an 8-K granting the CEO a 1.437M-share PSU award tied to $2.5B-$7.5B revenue and $244.70-$489.40 stock-price hurdles, and a Rebellions RebelPOD ZeroFlap AEC design-in. Today's +4.1% (to $231, +61.5% YTD, $38B cap) is continued post-earnings momentum/flow, not new evidence.

sources.label: Investing.com - Credo Q4 2026 earnings call transcript (beat, FY27 guide)
StockTitan - 8-K: FY26 revenue triples, CEO performance PSU award
MarketBeat - CRDO analyst price targets (post-earnings raises to $250-$300)
Yahoo Finance - CRDO quote and Q4 2026 earnings call highlights

verdicts.rankRationale: This page rewards picks-and-shovels names with smaller market visibility than the megacaps; at rank 1, $38B cap, +61.5% YTD and another +4% today on zero fresh news, CRDO is now fully discovered and consensus-owned (PTs $250-$300), so residual alpha is shrinking even as fundamentals shine.
verdicts.bull: FY26 revenue tripled to $1.34B and management guided >80% growth for FY27; AECs plus DustPhotonics SiPho/CPO optionality keep it the purest scale-out connectivity compounder.
verdicts.neutral: Spectacular execution is now matched by spectacular expectations; the CEO PSU hurdles ($2.5B-$7.5B revenue) telegraph the bull case the market already prices.
verdicts.bear: At ~28x forward sales with 3-4 customers each >10% of revenue, any hyperscaler order digestion or AEC share loss to optics compresses the multiple violently.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: FQ1 FY27 print (Sept) confirming the >80% FY27 growth path and new 10% customer ramps.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding unwind: post-rerating multiple compression on any guidance wobble or customer-concentration hiccup.

verdicts.rankRationale: The crowding penalty was already taken here (rank slid from 21 to 42 and the listing flags 'high multiple after repricing'); no new fundamental or flow information since June 6 justifies double-counting it, and the DustPhotonics SiPho/CPO option remains intact.
verdicts.bull: DustPhotonics gives CRDO a credible SiPho/CPO ramp on top of dominant AEC share, and FY27 >80% growth guidance funds the optical roadmap.
verdicts.neutral: Within a CPO-specific alpha lens CRDO is a cable/DSP company with optionality, not a CPO pure play; the rank already reflects both the quality and the repricing.
verdicts.bear: If CPO adoption accelerates at the switch level, copper AEC content per rack could peak sooner than the multiple assumes.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: DustPhotonics/SiPho product milestones or a named CPO-adjacent design win following the OFC 2026 portfolio debut.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Revenue concentration plus a premium multiple leave no cushion if a top customer pauses AEC orders.

verdicts.rankRationale: On the broad network-centrality list the bottleneck exposure (AECs, optical DSPs, scale-out bandwidth) is intact and arguably strengthened by FY27 guidance, but all of that news pre-dates June 6 and the rank already balances it against the $38B cap and 61.5% YTD rerating; today's +4% drift only nudges valuation recognition.
verdicts.bull: Record FY26 ($1.34B, ~206% growth) with >80% FY27 growth guided keeps CRDO among the highest-velocity nodes in AI scale-out connectivity.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis unchanged since the June 1 print; the market has fully internalized the beat via four PT raises, so centrality is high but no longer under-recognized.
verdicts.bear: A $38B company guiding off a $1.34B base is priced for flawless multi-customer execution; one soft quarter resets the YTD gain.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: New hyperscaler/neocloud AEC qualifications (e.g., more RebelPOD-style turnkey design-ins) broadening the customer base beyond 3-4 whales.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Customer concentration and AEC-to-optics substitution risk at next-gen rack architectures.
